replies.author: @DankKnutts
replies.text:
​​@Cindybin46 

Crude:
1. constructed in a rudimentary or makeshift way.


2. offensively coarse or explicit, especially in relation to sexual matters.

3. in a natural or raw state; not yet processed or refined.


So which meaning did you infer? That I'm not refined enough for standard? Or perhaps you meant I'm not structurally sound and therefore inferior to you? I didn't say it sexually but are are calling me a sexual offender? Or just offensive to you in general even though nothing was directed to you? Which way did you intend to insult me? Bitch!

replies.author: @chooselove2553
replies.text: @Cindybin46 Controlling language is a very messy thing. I can tell you that my aunt Karen thinks it is funny. Then again, she is a secure and sane person that doesn't let a "slur" bother her. I believe she grew up in the "sticks and stones will break my bones, but words will never hurt me" era.  This whole "words are violence" era doesn't compute for her, nor does it for me.  I say bring back Stoicism.  If it hurt your feelers, then adjust and realize people can be mean spirited and as long as they don't physically hurt you, you're going to be okay. Before you say "silence is violence" let me preemptively roll my eyes.

replies.text: @C@Cindybin46I understand that as a white woman myself.  I see it everyday online, can you or I  control it?  I’m going with no.  You know what I noticed?  People don’t come out and say it to my face, it’s online.  A bunch of cowards that are “brave” keyboard warriors.  Thing is, if it is a slur, and I am not sure it is,the more upset you are by it, the more prevalent it becomes, the more they use it to get under your skin.  Why give that satisfaction?  Instead of controlling people around you, control your response to it.  Yes people nowadays hate whiteness, but God made us all and He is still in control.  I choose to ignore, smile and pray for them.  Hoping that someday I will see my enemies in paradise.  I am not going to call everyone out on their language, my will controls nothing except me.  Neither does your will control anything but you.  It’s God’s will that matters.  If you don’t know what that is, I encourage you to read Matthew 22:36 -39 and also Romans 12:14.  There is power in wishing good will and praying for your enemies.  All else is just noise honey, just noise.  Be easy and know that God is in control.
